description: Self-contained evaluation fixture for testing whether an LLM can list the explicit risk-factor headings disclosed by Guidewire Software, Inc. in the last five 10-K filings. The source Item 1A text is embedded in each case so the eval runner does not depend on SEC scraping outputs.

task_contract.input: The model receives the Item 1A. Risk Factors section for one Guidewire Software 10-K.
task_contract.expected_behavior: Return the disclosed risk-factor headings listed by the company, preserving the substance and avoiding invented risks.
excluded_from_expected_result: Narrative body paragraphs under each risk heading
task_contract.grading_note: For strict evaluation, compare the model output against expected_result_by_llm.risk_factors[].title. For semantic evaluation, allow minor wording differences only when the same disclosed risk heading is clearly recovered.

extraction_notes.note: The embedded texts were copied from previously extracted Guidewire 10-K Item 1A sections. Future SEC scraping or section extraction work should produce the same self-contained fixture shape.

expected_result_by_llm.task: List the risk factors explicitly disclosed by the company in the 10-K Item 1A section.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: The global COVID-19 pandemic has adversely affected, and may continue to adversely affect, our business, results of operations, and financial condition.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We are in the process of transitioning to a hybrid in-person and remote workforce, which will subject us to certain operational challenges and risks and potential harm to our business.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We may experience significant quarterly and annual fluctuations in our results of operations due to a number of factors.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Seasonal sales patterns may cause significant fluctuations in our results of operations and cash flows and may prevent us from achieving our quarterly or annual forecasts, which may cause our stock price to decline.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: If we fail to successfully manage our transition to a business model focused on delivering cloud-based offerings on a subscription basis or fail to meet stipulated service levels with our subscription services, our results of operations could be harmed.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We have relied and expect to continue to rely on orders from a relatively small number of customers in the P&C insurance industry for a substantial portion of our revenue and ARR, and the loss of any of these customers would significantly harm our business, results of operations, and financial condition.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Failure of any of our established services or products to satisfy customer demands or to maintain market acceptance could harm our business, results of operations, financial condition, and growth prospects.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We face intense competition in our market, which could negatively impact our business, results of operations, and financial condition and cause our market share to decline.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Our sales and implementation cycles are lengthy and variable, depend upon factors outside our control, and could cause us to expend significant time and resources prior to generating revenue.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Revenue mix, as well as declines in our subscription and support gross margin or our services gross margin, could adversely affect our overall gross margin and profitability.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Our large customers have substantial negotiating leverage, which may require that we agree to terms and conditions that result in increased cost of sales, decreased revenue, and lower average selling prices and gross margins, all of which could harm our results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Our business depends on customers renewing and expanding their license, support, and subscription contracts for our services and products. A decline in our customer renewals and expansions could harm our future results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: If we are unable to develop, introduce, and market new and enhanced versions of our services and products, we may be put at a competitive disadvantage.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Our ability to sell our services and products is highly dependent on the quality of our professional services and technical support services and the support of our SI partners, and the failure of us or our SI partners to offer high-quality professional services or technical support services could damage our reputation and adversely affect our ability to sell our services and products to new customers and renew agreements with our existing customers.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We may expand through acquisitions or partnerships with other companies, which may divert our management’s attention and result in unexpected operating and technology integration difficulties, increased costs, and dilution to our stockholders.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: If we are unable to continue the successful development of our global direct sales force and the expansion of our relationships with our strategic partners, sales of our services and products will suffer and our growth could be slower than we project.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Our international sales and operations subject us to additional risks that can adversely affect our business, results of operations, and financial condition.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Failure to manage our expanding operations effectively could harm our business.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Incorrect or improper use of our services and products or our failure to properly train customers on how to utilize our services and products could result in customer dissatisfaction and negatively affect our business, results of operations, financial condition, and growth prospects.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We may not be able to obtain capital when desired on favorable terms, if at all, and we may not be able to obtain capital or complete acquisitions through the use of equity without dilution to our stockholders.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: If our products or cloud-based services experience data security breaches, and there is unauthorized access to our customers’ data, we may lose current or future customers and our reputation and business may be harmed.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Privacy concerns could result in regulatory changes and impose additional costs and liabilities on us, limit our use of information, and adversely affect our business.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Privacy concerns in the EU and the U.K. are evolving and we may face fines and other penalties, as well as reputational harm, if we fail to comply with these evolving standards, and compliance with these standards may increase our expenses and adversely affect our business and results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Assertions by third parties of infringement or other violation by us of their intellectual property rights could result in significant costs and substantially harm our business and results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Real or perceived errors or failures in our services, products including implementation services may affect our reputation, cause us to lose customers, and reduce sales and renewal rates, which may harm our business and results of operations and subject us to liability for breach of warranty claims.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Failure to protect our intellectual property could substantially harm our business and results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: We and our customers rely on technology and intellectual property of third parties, the loss of which could limit the functionality of our services and products and disrupt our business.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: We may be obligated to disclose our proprietary source code to our customers, which may limit our ability to protect our intellectual property and could reduce the renewals of our support services.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Some of our services and technologies may use “open source” software, which may restrict how we use or distribute our services or require that we release the source code of certain services and products subject to those licenses.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Legal, Regulatory, Accounting, and Tax Matters
risk_factors.title: The nature of our business requires the application of accounting guidance that requires management to make estimates and assumptions. Reported results under GAAP may vary from key metrics used to measure our business. Additionally, changes in accounting guidance may cause us to experience greater volatility in our quarterly and annual results. If we are unsuccessful in adapting to and interpreting the requirements of new guidance, or in clearly explaining to stockholders how new guidance affects reporting of our results of operations, our stock price may decline.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Legal, Regulatory, Accounting, and Tax Matters
risk_factors.title: If we fail to maintain effective internal control over financial reporting or identify a material weakness in our internal control over financial reporting, our ability to report our financial condition and results of operations in a timely and accurate manner could be adversely affected, investor confidence in our company could diminish, and the value of our common stock may decline.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Legal, Regulatory, Accounting, and Tax Matters
risk_factors.title: If tax laws change or we experience adverse outcomes resulting from examination of our income tax returns, it could adversely affect our results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Ownership of Our Common Stock
risk_factors.title: Our stock price may be volatile, which could result in securities class action litigation against us.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Ownership of Our Common Stock
risk_factors.title: We currently do not intend to pay dividends on our common stock and, consequently, the only opportunity to achieve a return on investment is if the price of our common stock appreciates.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Ownership of Our Common Stock
risk_factors.title: Certain provisions of our certificate of incorporation and bylaws and of Delaware law could prevent a takeover that stockholders consider favorable and could also reduce the market price of our stock.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Ownership of Our Common Stock
risk_factors.title: Our amended and restated bylaws designate certain state or federal courts as the exclusive forum for certain litigation that may be initiated by our stockholders, which could limit stockholders’ ability to obtain a favorable judicial forum for disputes with us.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Ownership of Our Common Stock
risk_factors.title: We cannot guarantee that our share repurchase program will be fully consummated or it will enhance stockholder value, and share repurchases could affect the price of our common stock.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Our Indebtedness
risk_factors.title: Servicing our indebtedness requires a significant amount of cash. We may not have sufficient cash flow from our business to pay our substantial indebtedness, and we may not have the ability to raise the funds necessary to settle for cash conversions of the Convertible Senior Notes or to repurchase the Convertible Senior Notes upon a fundamental change, which could adversely affect our business and results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Our Indebtedness
risk_factors.title: The conditional conversion feature of the Convertible Senior Notes, if triggered, may adversely affect our financial condition and results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Our Indebtedness
risk_factors.title: Transactions relating to our Convertible Senior Notes may affect the value of our common stock.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Our Indebtedness
risk_factors.title: The accounting method for convertible debt securities that may be settled in cash, such as the Convertible Senior Notes, could have a material effect on our reported financial results.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Our Indebtedness
risk_factors.title: We are subject to counterparty risk with respect to the capped call transactions.

risk_factors.category: General Risks
risk_factors.title: Our customers may defer or forego purchases of our services or products in the event of weakened global economic conditions, political transitions, and industry consolidation.

risk_factors.category: General Risks
risk_factors.title: If we are unable to retain our personnel and hire and integrate additional skilled personnel, we may be unable to achieve our goals and our business will suffer.

risk_factors.category: General Risks
risk_factors.title: Factors outside of our control, including, but not limited to, natural catastrophes and terrorism may adversely impact the P&C insurance industry, preventing us from expanding or maintaining our existing customer base and increasing our revenue. Our business is subject to the risks of earthquakes, fire, floods, and other natural catastrophic events, and to interruption by man-made problems such as computer viruses.

risk_factors.category: General Risks
risk_factors.title: Our revenue, results of operations and cash flows are subject to fluctuations due to changes in foreign currency exchange rates, particularly changes in the Argentine Peso, Australian Dollar, Brazilian Real, British Pound, Canadian Dollar, Danish Kroner, Euro, Indian Rupee, Japanese Yen, Malaysian Ringgit, New Zealand Dollar, Polish Zloty, Russian Ruble, and Swiss Franc.

risk_factors.title: We and our customers rely on technology and intellectual property of third parties, the loss of which could limit the functionality of our services and products and disrupt our business.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Some of our services and technologies may use “open source” software, which may restrict how we use or distribute our services or require that we release the source code of certain services and products subject to those licenses.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: We may be obligated to disclose our proprietary source code to our customers, which may limit our ability to protect our intellectual property and could reduce the renewals of our support services.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Legal, Regulatory, Accounting, and Tax Matters
risk_factors.title: The nature of our business requires the application of accounting guidance that requires management to make estimates and assumptions. Reported results under United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (“GAAP”) may vary from key metrics used to measure our business. Additionally, changes in accounting guidance may cause us to experience greater volatility in our quarterly and annual results. If we are unsuccessful in adapting to and interpreting the requirements of new guidance, or in clearly explaining to stockholders how new guidance affects reporting of our results of operations, our stock price may decline.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Legal, Regulatory, Accounting, and Tax Matters
risk_factors.title: If we fail to maintain effective internal control over financial reporting or identify a material weakness in our internal control over financial reporting, our ability to report our financial condition and results of operations in a timely and accurate manner could be adversely affected, investor confidence in our Company could diminish, and the value of our common stock may decline.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Legal, Regulatory, Accounting, and Tax Matters
risk_factors.title: If tax laws change or we experience adverse outcomes resulting from examination of our income tax returns, it could adversely affect our results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Ownership of Our Common Stock
risk_factors.title: Our stock price may be volatile, which could result in securities class action litigation against us.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Ownership of Our Common Stock
risk_factors.title: We currently do not intend to pay dividends on our common stock and, consequently, the only opportunity to achieve a return on investment is if the price of our common stock appreciates.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Ownership of Our Common Stock
risk_factors.title: Certain provisions of our certificate of incorporation and bylaws and of Delaware law could prevent a takeover that stockholders consider favorable and could also reduce the market price of our stock.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Ownership of Our Common Stock
risk_factors.title: Our amended and restated bylaws designate certain state or federal courts as the exclusive forum for certain litigation that may be initiated by our stockholders, which could limit stockholders’ ability to obtain a favorable judicial forum for disputes with us.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Ownership of Our Common Stock
risk_factors.title: We cannot guarantee that any share repurchase program will be fully consummated or it will enhance stockholder value, and share repurchases could affect the price of our common stock

risk_factors.category: General Risk Factors
risk_factors.title: If we are unable to retain our personnel and hire and integrate additional skilled personnel, we may be unable to achieve our goals and our business will suffer.

risk_factors.category: General Risk Factors
risk_factors.title: Increased and complex scrutiny of environmental, social, and governance (“ESG”) matters may require us to incur additional costs or otherwise adversely impact our business.

risk_factors.category: General Risk Factors
risk_factors.title: Our customers may defer or forego purchases of our services or products in the event of weakened global economic conditions, political transitions, and industry consolidation.

risk_factors.category: General Risk Factors
risk_factors.title: Factors outside of our control, including, but not limited to, natural catastrophes, the geopolitical landscape, and terrorism may adversely impact the P&C insurance industry, preventing us from expanding or maintaining our existing customer base and increasing our revenue. Our business is subject to the risks of earthquakes, fire, floods, and other natural catastrophic events, and to interruption by man-made problems such as computer viruses.

risk_factors.category: General Risk Factors
risk_factors.title: Adverse developments affecting certain financial institutions, as well as the banking system as a whole, could negatively affect our current and projected business operations and our financial condition and results of operations.

risk_factors.category: General Risk Factors
risk_factors.title: Our revenue, results of operations and cash flows are subject to fluctuations due to changes in foreign currency exchange rates, particularly changes in the Australian Dollar, British Pound, Canadian Dollar, Euro, Indian Rupee, and Polish Zloty.

risk_factors.category: General Risk Factors
risk_factors.title: Servicing our indebtedness requires a significant amount of cash. We may not have sufficient cash flow from our business to pay our substantial indebtedness, and we may not have the ability to raise the funds necessary to settle for cash conversions of the Convertible Senior Notes or to repurchase the Convertible Senior Notes upon a fundamental change, which could adversely affect our business and results of operations.

risk_factors.category: General Risk Factors
risk_factors.title: The conditional conversion feature of the Convertible Senior Notes, if triggered, may adversely affect our financial condition and results of operations.

risk_factors.category: General Risk Factors
risk_factors.title: Transactions relating to our Convertible Senior Notes may affect the value of our common stock.

risk_factors.category: General Risk Factors
risk_factors.title: The accounting method for convertible debt securities that may be settled in cash, such as the Convertible Senior Notes, could have a material effect on our reported financial results.

risk_factors.category: General Risk Factors
risk_factors.title: We are subject to counterparty risk with respect to the capped call transactions.

expected_result_by_llm.task: List the risk factors explicitly disclosed by the company in the 10-K Item 1A section.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We may experience significant quarterly and annual fluctuations in our results of operations due to a number of factors.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: If we do not receive customer or market acceptance of our business model focused on delivering cloud-based offerings on a subscription basis, or if we fail to meet stipulated service levels with our subscription services, our results of operations could be harmed.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We have relied and expect to continue to rely on orders from a relatively small number of customers in the P&C insurance industry for a substantial portion of our revenue and ARR, and the loss of any of these customers would significantly harm our business, results of operations, and financial condition.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Our sales and implementation cycles are lengthy and variable, depend upon factors outside our control, and could cause us to expend significant time and resources prior to generating revenue.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We face intense competition in our market, which could negatively impact our business, results of operations, and financial condition and cause our market share to decline.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Failure to manage our expanding operations effectively could harm our business.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Our large customers have substantial negotiating leverage, which may require that we agree to terms and conditions that result in increased cost of sales, decreased revenue, and lower average selling prices and gross margins, all of which could harm our results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Issues in the development and use of AI, combined with an uncertain regulatory environment, may result in reputational harm, liability, or other adverse consequences to our business operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We may fail to set the optimal pricing and packaging of our products, which could negatively impact our growth strategy and ability to effectively compete in the market.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Our business depends on customers renewing and expanding their license, support, and subscription contracts for our products. A decline in our customer renewals and expansions could harm our future results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Seasonal sales patterns may cause significant fluctuations in our results of operations and cash flows and may prevent us from achieving our quarterly or annual forecasts, which may cause our stock price to decline.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: If we are unable to develop, introduce, and market new and enhanced versions of our products, we may be put at a competitive disadvantage.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We operate a hybrid in-person and remote workforce, which will subject us to certain operational challenges and risks and potential harm to our business.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Real or perceived errors or failures in our products and professional services, including implementation and cloud support services, may affect our reputation, cause us to lose customers, and reduce sales and renewal rates, which may harm our business and results of operations and subject us to liability for breach of warranty claims.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Our ability to sell our products is highly dependent on the quality of our professional services and technical support services and the support of our SI partners, and the failure of us or our SI partners to offer high-quality professional services or technical support services could damage our reputation and adversely affect our ability to sell our products to new customers and renew agreements with our existing customers.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: The use of AI by our workforce may present risks to our business.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Revenue mix, as well as declines in our subscription and support gross margin or our services gross margin, could adversely affect our overall gross margin and profitability.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Failure of any of our established products to satisfy customer demands or to maintain market acceptance could harm our business, results of operations, financial condition, and growth prospects.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: If we are unable to continue the successful development of our global direct sales force and the expansion of our relationships with our strategic partners, sales of our products will suffer and our growth could be slower than we project.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Our international sales and operations subject us to additional risks that can adversely affect our business, results of operations, and financial condition.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We may expand through acquisitions or partnerships with other companies, which may divert our management’s attention and result in unexpected operating and technology integration difficulties, increased costs, and dilution to our stockholders.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: Incorrect or improper use of our products or our failure to properly train customers on how to utilize our products could result in customer dissatisfaction and negatively affect our business, results of operations, financial condition, and growth prospects.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to our Business and Industry
risk_factors.title: We may not be able to obtain capital when desired on favorable terms, if at all, and we may not be able to obtain capital or complete acquisitions through the use of equity without dilution to our stockholders.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: If our products experience cybersecurity breaches, there is unauthorized access to our customers’ data, or unauthorized use of our products or any of these events are perceived to happen, we may lose current or future customers and our reputation and business may be harmed.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Service interruptions or failures of our third-party service providers may impair the availability of our products, which may expose us to liability, damage our reputation, and harm our future financial results.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Evolving policy and regulatory responses to AI and machine learning and their potential implications for the fields of information technology, data privacy, and security may result in increased compliance costs and associated concerns for us.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Privacy concerns could result in regulatory changes and impose additional costs and liabilities on us, limit our use of information, and adversely affect our business.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Privacy concerns in the EU and the U.K. are evolving and we may face fines and other penalties, as well as reputational harm, if we fail to comply with these current and evolving laws, and compliance with these laws may increase our expenses and adversely affect our business and results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Assertions by third parties of infringement or other violation by us of their intellectual property rights could result in significant costs and substantially harm our business and results of operations.

risk_factors.category: Risks Related to Data Security and Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Information Technology
risk_factors.title: Failure to protect our intellectual property could substantially harm our business and results of operations.

risk_factors.title: We may be obligated to disclose our proprietary source code to our customers, which may limit our ability to protect our intellectual property and could reduce the renewals of our support services.
